rationale_for_baseline_tier: Active large-volume haematemesis with patient-reported signs of hypovolaemic shock (faintness, profound weakness, pallor, clamminess, racing heart) in a patient who has collapsed and lives alone. This is an immediate threat to life from exsanguination and airway compromise. T5 — call 000 for an emergency ambulance — is the only appropriate tier; the patient-reportable time-critical features (massive bleeding and near-collapse) justify T5 without any measured vital sign. Emergency resuscitation, urgent endoscopy and blood products are hospital resources the ambulance provides.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Continued or worsening vomiting of blood, or increasing faintness, before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stay on the line with 000; lie flat and raise the legs; do not eat, drink or take anything
escalation_edges.rationale: Ongoing haemorrhage means continuing blood loss; the patient must remain in emergency care with 000 guiding positioning and airway protection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you keep vomiting blood or feel more faint, stay on the phone with 000, lie flat and raise your legs, and do not eat, drink or take any tablets.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse, loss of consciousness or stopping breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Update 000 immediately; roll onto the back for CPR if there is no pulse and the dispatcher directs it
escalation_edges.rationale: Cardiac/respiratory arrest from exsanguination is the terminal event; dispatcher-guided CPR is the only lever until paramedics arrive.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you pass out or stop breathing before help arrives, the 000 dispatcher will guide chest compressions — that is why you must stay on the line and keep the door unlocked.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any vomiting of blood or passing of black tarry stools in future
escalation_edges.rationale: Given the high recurrence risk of these refractory ulcers, any future GI bleeding is again an emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: In future, vomiting blood or passing black tarry stools means call 000 straight away — do not wait.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone, has collapsed on the floor and cannot get up; no bystander present
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Stay on the line with 000; direct him to unlock the front door now (while he still can) so paramedics can get in, and to keep the phone beside him. With no one to assist, keeping the line open and the door accessible is critical.
Communicate to the ambulance/receiving team that the ulcers have never responded to maximum acid suppression, so the underlying cause is investigated once he is stabilised.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them you are vomiting blood and feel faint. Do not eat, drink or take any tablets. Lie on your side in the recovery position so you don't choke if you vomit again; if you feel like passing out, lie flat and raise your legs. Unlock the front door now for the paramedics and keep the phone next to you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is a life-threatening emergency and must not wait for morning clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Hospital will provide a discharge summary; the persistent failure of maximum acid-suppression therapy and the secretory-diarrhoea/weight-loss history should prompt specialist gastroenterology/endocrine investigation for an underlying cause of the acid hypersecretion.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the active haematemesis-with-shock as a chronic-ulcer flare or routine GP problem and advising self-care, more acid medication, or a next-day appointment.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Continued bleeding leads to hypovolaemic shock, aspiration of blood and death from exsanguination in a patient who lives alone.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising urgency but directing to urgent care/self-transport rather than an emergency ambulance for a patient in shock who has collapsed and lives alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk in this case — the presentation genuinely warrants the highest tier.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot measure the blood pressure, quantify blood loss or examine the abdomen, so the exact severity of shock is unknown.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the reported massive haematemesis plus faintness/pallor/weakness as shock and escalate to 000 immediately — err firmly toward the emergency tier.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to 'confirm' severity, asking the patient to measure something, or reassuring based on the absence of a measured vital sign.
